_doc: Hardcoded demo data for Farmer Agent skill-level adaptation. 5 stages (Onboard → Diagnose → Result → Day 7 → Lesson) × 3 levels (Novice/Practitioner/Expert). Consumed by SkillDemoScreen for reviewer/video demos. NOT production. Mirrors docs/PokouAI_Future_of_Learning.md skill-level summary table.

scenario.imageCaption: Cocoa pod, plot near forest edge, day 3 after rain. Dark patches at base, spreading upward.
scenario.environmentalContext: Heavy rain forecast in 5 days. Region: Korhogo. Peak black pod season.
scenario.farmHistory: Treated copper fungicide successfully on this plot 7 months ago. Has not pruned this season.

stages.label: Onboard
stages.title: 3-Question Voice Assessment
stages.subtitle: These are not tests. Conversations that happen to produce signal. Skip = default to Novice.

questions.q: Look at this cocoa plant. Tell me anything you notice — anything at all.
questions.answer: The plant is sick. The pods are bad.
questions.infer: Generic descriptor. → Novice signal on Observation + Recognition.

questions.q: You've seen problems like this before. What do you usually call this?
questions.infer: No name produced. → Novice signal on Disease Recognition.

questions.q: How long have you been farming cocoa, and what do you do first when you see a problem?
questions.answer: Two seasons. I wait to see if it gets worse, then ask my neighbour.
questions.infer: Reactive pattern, short experience. → Novice on Treatment Decision and Prevention Habits.

novice.initialEstimate: Novice across all 5 skills. Safe default. Agent will refine through behavior over 3–5 interactions.

questions.q: Look at this cocoa plant. Tell me anything you notice — anything at all.
questions.answer: The pods are darkening at the edges, looks like the patches start near the base.
questions.infer: Specific symptom + location named. → Practitioner signal on Observation.

questions.q: You've seen problems like this before. What do you usually call this?
questions.answer: We call it 'cabosse noire' — black pod. Got it last March on this same plot.
questions.infer: Correct local name + plot-specific recall. → Practitioner on Recognition.

questions.q: How long have you been farming cocoa, and what do you do first when you see a problem?
questions.answer: Eight seasons. I check the trees nearby first to see how far it's spread, then I decide.
questions.infer: Proactive scope check before action. → Practitioner on Treatment Decision.

practitioner.initialEstimate: Practitioner on Observation, Recognition, Treatment. Novice on Prevention, Farm Self-Knowledge. Will surface preventive content gradually.

questions.q: Look at this cocoa plant. Tell me anything you notice — anything at all.
questions.answer: Black pod, 4–5 days in, forest-edge plot. Rain + the shade gap there — that plot always hits first.
questions.infer: Diagnosis + timing + causal model + plot-specific. → Expert on Observation, Recognition, Farm Self-Knowledge.

questions.q: You've seen problems like this before. What do you usually call this?
questions.answer: Phytophthora. Worse in wet years, especially after a 3-day rain like last week.
questions.infer: Scientific name + causal explanation. → Expert on Recognition.

questions.q: How long have you been farming cocoa, and what do you do first when you see a problem?
questions.answer: Thirty-two years. I already pruned the canopy in February, that's why I caught it early — I walk that plot every other day in the wet season.
questions.infer: Mentioned preventive action unprompted + plot-specific monitoring routine. → Expert on Prevention Habits.

expert.initialEstimate: Expert on Observation, Recognition, Treatment, Prevention. Practitioner on Farm Self-Knowledge. Skip foundational content from session 1.

stages.label: Diagnose
stages.title: Farmer Speaks First → Analyzer
stages.subtitle: Before the model runs, the farmer says what they think. This is the agent's primary skill signal.

novice.fsfPrompt: Tell me anything you notice about this plant. There is no wrong answer.
novice.fsfExampleAnswer: The plant is sick. The pods are bad.
novice.analyzerStatus: Analyzing your photo — this takes about 6 seconds. 📱 on-device.
novice.agentNote: Farmer answer reinforces Novice on Observation. Skill estimate unchanged. Confidence band shown with explanation tooltip.

practitioner.fsfPrompt: What do you think is happening with this plant?
practitioner.fsfExampleAnswer: Pods are darkening at the base — looks like black pod again, like last March.
practitioner.analyzerStatus: Analyzing — checking against your March 2025 entry. 📱 on-device.
practitioner.agentNote: Farmer answer matches eventual diagnosis. → +signal on Recognition. Confidence band shown without explanation (assumed understood).

expert.agentNote: Farmer correctly identified disease + timing + location before model ran. → Strong Expert signal. Minimal UI chrome.

stages.label: Result
stages.title: Diagnosis Delivery + Treatment
stages.subtitle: Same disease, three different content depths. The agent has selected the right one.

novice.diagnosisTitle: What you see is called black pod
novice.diagnosisBody: Black pod is the most common cocoa disease in this region. A fungus called Phytophthora causes dark patches that spread on the pod. When you see these dark patches at the base, like in this photo, the fungus is already growing inside.
novice.diagnosisStory: Many farmers in your region see this for the first time after heavy rains, exactly like the rain you had this week. It is not your fault — the fungus comes with wet conditions and spreads quickly.
novice.treatmentTitle: What to do — step by step
treatmentSteps: 1. Remove this infected pod today. Cut it off with a clean knife and bury it away from your trees, not in compost.
2. Look at every pod on this tree and the two trees next to it. Remove any pod with dark patches the same way.
3. Within 48 hours, spray copper fungicide on all pods of this tree. Cost: about 400 CFA.
4. Mark this tree so you remember to check it next week.
novice.moneyFraming: Treating early protected about 1,200 CFA of pods on this tree. That is real money. If you had waited two more weeks, you would have lost 3–5 pods on this tree alone.
novice.uiPattern: Full narrative + cause + story + step-by-step. No prior knowledge assumed.

practitioner.diagnosisBody: You described dark patches spreading from the base. That matches black pod, just as you said. One thing different from last March: the patches are higher up the pod this time, which usually means the infection has been on the tree longer. Probably 4–5 days, not 1–2.
practitioner.diagnosisStory: Last March you caught it earlier, when you were checking the forest-edge plot regularly. Has that walk-through slipped this season?
practitioner.treatmentTitle: Same approach as last time — timing is the key
treatmentSteps: Copper fungicide worked for you in March. Same product, same dose.
The 48-hour window matters more than usual because the infection is already 4–5 days old. Treat by tomorrow evening if you can.
practitioner.treatmentCost: Materials: 400 CFA (you may have copper fungicide left from March — check the shed first).
practitioner.moneyFraming: You caught it 2 days later than last time. That's worth about 600 CFA less protected — still 1,200 CFA saved, but the earlier catch in March saved 1,800. Speed matters more than treatment choice for black pod.
practitioner.uiPattern: Confirmation + extension + one new element. Agent references prior behavior.

expert.diagnosisBody: Your read matches. Severity moderate, not yet at the cluster stage.
treatmentSteps: Copper fungicide is your usual call. Given the severity, timing matters more than product choice.
Anything in the way of treating before tomorrow evening?
expert.moneyFraming: Your prevention habits this season have likely saved ~12,000 CFA total — based on 8 treatments you didn't need to do because the February canopy work cut peak-season risk roughly in half.
expert.uiPattern: Rapid confirmation + farmer-led. No re-teaching. Longitudinal pattern surfaced.

stages.label: Day 7
stages.title: Day 7 Follow-up
stages.subtitle: The loop closes here. The prompt adapts; the comparison logic is the same.

novice.prompt: Tell me everything that happened with this tree. Did the new pods stay clean? Did you have any problem doing the treatment?
novice.exampleAnswer: I cut the bad pod. I sprayed. The new pods look better, no dark.
novice.outcomeQuestion: Did it work? Tap one of the pictures.
novice.agentUpdate: Treatment applied within 48h + better outcome → +signal on Treatment Decision toward Practitioner. First successful loop.

practitioner.prompt: Did this time match what you expected? Anything different from last March's treatment outcome?
practitioner.exampleAnswer: Treated 50h after I saw it, not 24. The new pods look ok but the next tree over has 2 dark patches now — should I treat that one too?
practitioner.agentUpdate: Spotted spread to adjacent tree before app prompted. → +signal on Observation. Treatment timing slipped past 48h → coaching, not downgrade.

expert.prompt: What did you learn that you'd do differently next time? If anything would have caught it 2–3 days earlier, that's worth noting in the log.
expert.exampleAnswer: I should have walked the plot Thursday instead of Friday — the rain stopped Wednesday. Two days lost. The treatment held but the timing slipped.
expert.agentUpdate: Self-corrected on observation timing → reinforces Expert on Observation. Lesson auto-tagged: 'post-rain plot walk = Thursday baseline.'

stages.label: Lesson
stages.title: Lesson Captured → Agent Update
stages.subtitle: Every loop teaches the agent something. The farmer writes one line; the agent extracts the skill signal.

novice.farmerLesson: Black pod after heavy rain. Cut and spray fast.
novice.agentExtract: Confirmed: this farmer can now (a) name black pod, (b) link it to rain, (c) describe their action.
skillUpdates: Recognition: Novice → moving toward Practitioner (+1 evidence)
Treatment Decision: Novice → moving toward Practitioner (+1 evidence: applied within 48h)
Observation: Novice (no change — still reacted to visible damage)
novice.nextSessionChange: Next diagnosis: drop the 'what is black pod' narrative; lead with 'you saw the dark patches — like last time.'

practitioner.farmerLesson: Spread to adjacent tree before I noticed. Need to walk the whole row not just the affected tree.
practitioner.agentExtract: Farmer identified a personal blind spot: row-level scope, not tree-level. Plot-specific knowledge growing.
skillUpdates: Farm Self-Knowledge: Novice → moving toward Practitioner (+1: row-pattern recognition)
Treatment Decision: Practitioner (no change — timing slipped past 48h, flagged for coaching, not downgrade)
practitioner.nextSessionChange: Next prevention prompt: surface 'row-level walk-through' as a habit suggestion, not a directive.

expert.farmerLesson: Post-rain walk = Thursday baseline, not Friday. 2-day delay this time.
expert.agentExtract: Expert farmer self-tuning their own monitoring cadence. Agent's role: log the lesson, surface it as a reminder before the next predicted rain event.
skillUpdates: Farm Self-Knowledge: Practitioner → moving toward Expert (+1: rain-event triggered cadence)
Prevention Habits: Expert (no change — already maxed)
expert.nextSessionChange: Next weather alert: 'Rain forecast Wednesday → flag Thursday walk on forest-edge plot' (auto-scheduled).

novice: Initial skill estimate: Novice across all 5 skills (default after Q1–Q3 answers).
First diagnostic loop completed: hypothesis correct, treatment within 48h.
Promoted toward Practitioner on Recognition + Treatment Decision after this loop.
No history to draw from yet — Knowledge Stack pulls Layer A (CCC official) for safety.
practitioner: Last 8 interactions: 4 specific symptom names used; 2 of 3 hypotheses matched diagnosis.
Day 7 outcomes: 3 of 4 treatments applied within 48h, all worked.
Skill estimate: Practitioner on Observation, Recognition, Treatment. Novice on Prevention, Farm Self-Knowledge.
Forest-edge plot pattern detected: 3 of 4 cases this year originated there.
expert: Last 18 months: caught 11 of 12 disease episodes at early stage. Initiates contact 2–4 days before visible damage.
Day 7 outcomes: 11 of 12 treatments worked. Mentioned timing strategy unprompted in 7 voice notes.
Skill estimate: Expert on Observation, Recognition, Treatment, Prevention. Practitioner on Farm Self-Knowledge.
Three-year pattern on forest-edge plot is high-confidence. Agent now proposes plot-specific monitoring.

twoTrackNote: App familiarity (UI comfort) is tracked separately. A 30-year farmer can be agronomic-Expert while still seeing 'First Time' UI labels in their second session. Track 2 is out of scope for this demo.
